verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a multi-pass transmembrane protein (6 TM helices) that resides on the Golgi apparatus, endosomes, and phagophore assembly sites, where it functions as a phospholipid scramblase to support autophagosome biogenesis. Its localization is endomembrane-restricted under baseline conditions. Checking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented stress-induced PM translocation; ATG9A cycles among endomembranes but not to the cell surface; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no evidence of surface display in any lineage; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — ATG9A cycles through early/recycling endosomes but is not reported to reach the PM via lysosomal exocytosis; (4) dual_localization — surface biotinylation or flow cytometry data on intact cells showing a PM pool is absent from the literature; (5) stable_surface_attachment — it is not secreted and does not associate with a surface-anchored partner. No therapeutic programs targeting ATG9A at the cell surface are known. The protein is an endomembrane-resident multi-pass TM protein with no credible surface pool.
